days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Exploring Asakusa and Local Cuisine

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Get through immigration and baggage claim. Look for the Airport Limousine Bus terminal for transport to your hotel.

timeline.title: Check in to The Gate Hotel Asakusa
timeline.detail: Drop your luggage at this stylish hotel near Senso-ji Temple, offering modern amenities and a rooftop terrace with spectacular views.
timeline.mapQuery: The Gate Hotel Asakusa, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Lunch at Asakusa Kikko
timeline.detail: Savor authentic tempura in a cozy setting near Senso-ji Temple, known for its fresh ingredients and delectable flavors.

timeline.title: Senso-ji Temple Visit
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo’s oldest temple, renowned for the iconic Kaminarimon Gate, and admire the beautiful architecture and gardens.

timeline.title: Nakamise Shopping Street
timeline.detail: Stroll through this vibrant shopping street leading to Senso-ji, filled with traditional snacks and souvenirs.

timeline.title: Dinner at Yoshinoya
timeline.detail: Indulge in a delicious gyudon (beef bowl) at this popular fast-food chain, known for its quick service and hearty meals.

timeline.title: Evening River Cruise
timeline.detail: Take a relaxing river cruise along the Sumida River, enjoying beautiful night views of Tokyo's skyline.

localTips: Purchase a Suica card at the airport for easy transport on trains and buses.
Consider dining during off-peak hours to avoid long waits.
Be aware of peak hours on public transport to avoid crowds.

days.title: Exploring Tokyo
days.subtitle: Shibuya, Harajuku, and Shinjuku

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hoshino Coffee
timeline.detail: Enjoy a fluffy pancake breakfast at this popular chain, famous for its attention to detail and unique coffee blends.

timeline.title: Shibuya Crossing
timeline.detail: Experience the hustle of one of the busiest pedestrian crossings in the world, capturing the electric atmosphere of Tokyo.

timeline.title: Shopping in Shibuya 109
timeline.detail: Explore this iconic shopping center filled with trendy boutiques and unique fashion items popular among Japanese youth.

timeline.title: Lunch at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Savor delicious tonkotsu ramen at this unique restaurant where you eat in individual booths for maximized taste and focus.

timeline.title: Takeshita Street in Harajuku
timeline.detail: Wander this colorful street filled with trendy shops, unique fashion, and quirky fashion items that are quintessential to Harajuku culture.

timeline.title: Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Relax in the serene forest surrounding the Meiji Shrine, an important Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken.

timeline.title: Dinner at Omoide Yokocho
timeline.detail: Explore the narrow alley lined with traditional izakayas serving delicious yakitori and other Japanese comfort food.

timeline.title: Explore Golden Gai
timeline.detail: Visit this vibrant nightlife district famous for its tiny bars and the opportunity to interact with friendly locals.

localTips: Use the Tokyo Metro app for the best routes and transfer information.
Take cash for smaller izakayas; some do not accept cards.
Visit a convenience store for unique snacks and drinks.

days.title: Day Trip to Nikko
days.subtitle: Nature and Culture

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Fuel up for your day trip with a traditional Japanese breakfast at your hotel, featuring grilled fish, rice, and miso soup.

timeline.title: Travel to Nikko
timeline.detail: Take the Tobu Limited Express from Asakusa Station to Nikko, enjoying scenic views along the route. Book your tickets in advance.

timeline.title: Arrive in Nikko
timeline.detail: Begin your exploration of this UNESCO World Heritage site, known for its beautiful nature and elaborate shrines.

timeline.title: Toshogu Shrine
timeline.detail: Visit the ornate Toshogu Shrine, the final resting place of Tokugawa Ieyasu, where you can admire the stunning carvings and architecture.

timeline.title: Lunch at Hippari Dako
timeline.detail: Try yuba (tofu skin) and other local delicacies at this popular eatery known for its cozy atmosphere and delicious dishes.

timeline.title: Kegon Falls
timeline.detail: Witness the breathtaking Kegon Falls, one of Japan's most famous waterfalls, and take the elevator down to the viewing platform.

timeline.title: Return to Tokyo
timeline.detail: Take the Tobu Limited Express back to Tokyo, arriving in the evening.

timeline.title: Dinner at Uobei Sushi
timeline.detail: Enjoy a fun and fast sushi experience at Uobei, where you can order via touch screens, and your food arrives on a conveyor belt.

localTips: Purchase a Nikko Pass for unlimited travel and discounts at attractions.
Be mindful of the last train times when returning to Tokyo.
Wear comfortable shoes as there will be a lot of walking.

days.title: Last Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Relaxing in Shinjuku and Departure

timeline.title: Breakfast at Eggs 'n Things
timeline.detail: Indulge in a varied breakfast menu featuring pancakes and omelets in this famous Hawaiian-themed café.

timeline.title: Visit Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
timeline.detail: Relax in this beautiful park featuring traditional Japanese, English, and French landscapes – a tranquil escape from the city hustle.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Lunch at Tonkatsu Wako
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty tonkatsu meal at this renowned chain, serving crispy pork cutlets with a variety of side dishes.

timeline.title: Shopping at Takashimaya Times Square
timeline.detail: Explore this massive shopping complex in Shinjuku, offering everything from luxury goods to quirky Japanese souvenirs.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Check out
timeline.detail: Head back to the hotel to collect your belongings and check out before your departure.

timeline.title: Travel to Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Take the Airport Limousine Bus from your hotel to Narita, allowing time for airport check-in and security.

localTips: Leave for the airport early to avoid missing your flight.
Take a few moments to enjoy the rooftop terrace at your hotel before leaving.
Check for last-minute souvenirs at Takashimaya’s basement food floor.

budget.note: Return flight from Singapore to Narita
Average ¥20,000/night at The Gate Hotel Asakusa
For all train and bus fares during trip
Round trip train fare on Tobu Limited Express
For gifts and keepsakes from Japan
Coverage for the duration of the trip

urgent.label: Book Tobu Limited Express train to Nikko
urgent.note: Tickets are limited, book in advance.
